item.title: Verdict
item.description: Final Verdict Yes. This project is good for interview if it stays small, ticket-led, and honest about being a home lab built in a trial tenant.

item.title: UK Role Alignment
item.description: Summary This project is designed to match the day-to-day shape of junior UK roles that mention Microsoft 365, Entra ID, Intune, Windows endpoints, and support workflows.

item.title: Final Project Scope
item.description: Summary The final version of this project is a small, ticket-driven Microsoft 365 endpoint support lab built in one tenant with one Windows 11 endpoint.

item.title: Ticket Scenarios
item.description: Summary These ticket scenarios are the core of the project.

item.title: Evidence Checklist
item.description: Summary Capture only evidence that proves the workflow.

item.title: Interview Pack
item.description: Summary This interview pack is designed to help explain the project clearly in 10-15 minutes without overselling it.

item.title: CV Bullets
item.description: 3 Credible CV Bullets Built a small Microsoft 365 endpoint support home lab in a trial tenant using one Windows 11 VM, covering user onboarding, Microsoft Entra join, Intune enrollment, basic policy assignment, app deployment, and access removal.

item.title: Microsoft 365 Endpoint Support
item.description: Summary This project is a small, interview-defensible Microsoft 365 endpoint support lab.

item.title: Knowledge Base - Failed Sign-In
item.description: Symptom The user reports that they cannot sign in to Microsoft 365 and receives a sign-in failure.

item.title: Knowledge Base - Non-Compliant Device
item.description: Symptom W11-M365-ENDP01 shows as non-compliant or stops showing the expected compliant result in Intune.
